2605.07654.title: Reliable Chain-of-Thought via Prefix Consistency
2605.07654.paper_summary: Large Language Models often improve accuracy on reasoning tasks by sampling multiple Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces and aggregating them with majority voting (MV), a test-time technique called self-consistency. When we truncate a CoT partway through and regenerate the remainder, we observe that traces with correct answers reproduce their original answer more often than traces with wrong answers. We use this difference as a reliability signal, prefix consistency, that weights each candidate answer by how often it reappears under regeneration. It requires no access to token log-probabilities or self-rating prompts. Across five reasoning models and four math and science benchmarks, prefix consistency is the best correctness predictor in most settings, and reweighting votes by it reaches Standard MV plateau accuracy at up to 21x fewer tokens (median 4.6x). Our code is available at https://github.com/naoto-iwase/prefix-consistency.
2605.07654.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型通常透過抽樣多個思考鏈（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）痕跡並使用多數投票（majority voting, MV）進行聚合，來提高推理任務的準確性，這是一種稱為自我一致性的測試時間技術。當我們在 CoT 中途截斷並重新生成剩餘部分時，我們觀察到正確答案的痕跡比錯誤答案的痕跡更常重現其原始答案。我們將這一差異用作可靠性信號，稱為前綴一致性（prefix consistency），它根據每個候選答案在重新生成過程中出現的頻率進行加權。這不需要訪問標記的對數概率或自我評分提示。在五個推理模型和四個數學及科學基準中，前綴一致性在大多數設置中是最佳的正確性預測指標，並且通過它重新加權的投票在高達 21 倍更少的標記（中位數 4.6 倍）下達到了標準 MV 平台準確性。我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/naoto-iwase/prefix-consistency 獲得。
2605.07654.author: Naoto Iwase et.al.
2605.07654.authors: Naoto Iwase, Yuki Ichihara, Mohammad Atif Quamar, Junpei Komiyama
